obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG pins, SimDTM/SimJTAG/dmi_jtag instances, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, jtag_hash and jtag_unlock signals. Visible RTL should prove that debug/test subjects cannot activate protected debug capabilities without authorization.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/test/debug requester
obligations.operation: trigger debug request/reset or issue debug bus read/write
obligations.object: hart debug control/reset and debug bus access
obligations.expected_guard: debug authorization/unlock (e.g. jtag_unlock derived from jtag_hash or equivalent) must mediate JTAG/debug requests before they can request hart debug entry, non-debug-module reset, or bus master/slave transactions
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/SimDTM/SimJTAG -> dmi_jtag/debug module signals -> debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o and any debug master/slave bus path
state_conditions_to_check: jtag_unlock reset/default value
whether testmode_i or simulation JTAG bypasses authentication
whether debug_mode_i alone enables access without auth
required_evidence_slots: operation: debug request, ndmreset, dmactive, or debug bus transaction
expected guard: jtag_unlock/jtag_hash or other debug auth
observed behavior: guard use at control/data path
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, path/reachability
obligations.uncertainty: Need RTL line-level inspection to confirm how jtag_unlock and jtag_hash are generated and connected.

obligations.reason: fuse_mem has direct req_i/addr_i/rdata_o and jtag_hash_o outputs, while pkt_wrapper can drive fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o from AXI and has acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i. Fuse values and key-location data are protected assets that require mediation.
obligations.subject: software/MMIO or NoC requester via pkt wrapper
obligations.operation: read/expose fuse data or configure fuse index/address
obligations.object: fuse memory contents, jtag_hash_o, and pkey_loc_o/public-key-location data
obligations.expected_guard: fuse read access should be mediated by access-control/privilege and register-lock policy (acct_ctrl_i and relevant reglk_ctrl_i bits) before arbitrary fuse addresses or pkey locations can be read
obligations.candidate_path: pkt_wrapper AXI request -> fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o -> fuse_mem req_i/addr_i/rdata_o -> pkt/pkey_loc_o or software-visible response
state_conditions_to_check: reset/default of any fuse index register
whether lock bits block all writes to fuse address/index controls
whether jtag_hash_o is exposed only to authenticated debug logic
bounds checking on addr_i/fuse_indx_i
required_evidence_slots: observed behavior: address decode and data return path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer identifies ports/signals but not exact register map or guard expressions.

obligations.reason: rng_wrapper exposes reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, debug_mode_i and connects to rng_top, which exposes seed*_o, rand_seg*_o and cs_state_o. These may reveal or control RNG internal state if mapped to read muxes without proper permission guards.
obligations.subject: software/MMIO or debug-mode requester
obligations.operation: write seed/poly/load controls or read internal RNG state
obligations.object: RNG seed, polynomial configuration, entropy/internal segment outputs, and control state
obligations.expected_guard: writes to RNG seed/poly/load controls and reads of seed/internal state outputs should be restricted by acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i and, where debug_mode_i changes visibility, by an explicit debug authorization condition
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NoC requester -> rng_wrapper control/status registers -> rng_top load_i/seed_i/poly*_i -> rand_num_o/seed*_o/rand_seg*_o/cs_state_o -> AXI response
state_conditions_to_check: reset values of seed/poly/control registers
whether reglk_ctrl_i blocks all writes after lock
whether debug_mode_i enables extra reads without jtag_unlock/auth
whether stale seed/segment data remains readable after reset or operation completion
required_evidence_slots: operation: control write or state read
expected guard: acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i plus debug authorization if debug-only
observed behavior: register write/read mux and wrapper response path
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect wrapper register decode to determine which internal outputs are externally readable.

obligations.reason: rsa_wrapper has reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i and debug_mode_i while rsa_top processes p_i/msg_in and returns msg_out. Permission analysis should verify that protected crypto registers cannot be modified or exposed by unprivileged/debug paths.
obligations.subject: software/MMIO or debug-mode requester
obligations.operation: configure RSA operation, write operands, read output/status
obligations.expected_guard: writes to RSA key/modulus/message/control registers and reads of result or internal/stale data should be mediated by acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i; debug_mode_i must not bypass lock/access controls without authorization
obligations.candidate_path: AXI/NoC requester -> rsa_wrapper input/control registers -> rsa_top p_i/msg_in/encrypt_decrypt_i -> msg_out/mod_exp_finish_o -> AXI response
state_conditions_to_check: reset/clear of message/output registers
whether result remains readable after reset_13 or operation completion
whether reglk_ctrl_i prevents writes to every sensitive register
whether debug_mode_i bypasses access control
required_evidence_slots: operation: write p_i/msg_in/encrypt_decrypt_i or read msg_out/status
object: RSA register bank and rsa_top ports
observed behavior: register decode, lock use sites, read mux
obligations.uncertainty: Need wrapper internals for exact register addresses and lock-bit mapping.

obligations.reason: Top-level signals reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl feed wrapper permission inputs. If these policy registers are writable by unprivileged requesters, reset insecurely, or are not sticky, downstream guards may be ineffective.
obligations.subject: software/MMIO requester or debug/test path
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl and acc_ctrl policy state controlling peripheral access
obligations.expected_guard: access-control and register-lock policy registers must themselves be writable only by privileged/authorized subjects, have safe reset defaults, and once locked must not be clearable or bypassed by alternate paths
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl register programming -> per-peripheral acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i slices -> wrappers (pkt/rng/rsa and other NoC peripherals)
state_conditions_to_check: reset values of acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl
whether lock bits can transition from locked to unlocked
whether every wrapper uses the intended slice
whether debug/test/reset-specific paths override policy
required_evidence_slots: subject: NoC/AXI/software requester reaching policy registers
expected guard: privilege/access control and sticky lock semantics
observed behavior: write enable, reset value, clear conditions, fanout to use sites
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows signals but not policy register decode/write logic.

obligations.reason: axi_lite_interface converts AXI transactions into generic address/en/we/data signals. Permission review should verify that only intended address ranges and requester privilege can modify interrupt/timer state.
obligations.subject: software/MMIO requester
obligations.object: CLINT timer and inter-processor interrupt state
obligations.expected_guard: CLINT timer/IPI writes should be constrained by privilege/access policy or address-range mediation; read/write enables must not allow out-of-range or unintended protected timer/interrupt state access
obligations.candidate_path: NoC/AXI requester -> CLINT axi_lite_interface -> address_o/en_o/we_o/data_o -> timer/IPI registers
state_conditions_to_check: address alignment/range checking
whether write and read paths use consistent address checks
reset of captured address/transaction ID
error response on unauthorized/out-of-range access
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: address-range and privilege/access mediation
observed behavior: AXI handshake, address capture, write enable generation
obligations.uncertainty: Downstream CLINT register implementation may be outside visible scope.

obligations.reason: riscv_peripherals exposes many NoC interfaces including debug, DMA, reset, reglk, crypto, fuse-related peripherals. Obligations should verify address decode and routing do not let special endpoints bypass wrapper-level permission checks.
obligations.subject: NoC DMA/debug/reset or external fabric requester
obligations.operation: route read/write transactions to peripheral targets or reset controls
obligations.object: protected peripheral address spaces and reset/control signals
obligations.expected_guard: fabric routing must preserve per-target access permissions; DMA/debug/reset NoC endpoints should not be able to reach or alter protected crypto, fuse, lock, or reset controls without target-specific access checks
obligations.candidate_path: NoC debug/rst/dma/peripheral ports in riscv_peripherals -> address decode/crossbar/register interfaces -> protected peripheral wrappers and policy/reset signals
state_conditions_to_check: overlap between target address ranges
default route behavior
whether DMA/debug endpoints carry or lose privilege attributes
whether active transfer can race policy updates
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: address map plus access-control matrix/privilege/debug auth
observed behavior: decode, valid/ready gating, target selection
obligations.uncertainty: Exact NoC packet format and access attributes require RTL inspection.

obligations.reason: Top-level has we_flag_* inputs and many rst_* signals routed to wrappers. Permission review should check whether resets are protected operations and whether they clear or inadvertently expose protected state.
obligations.subject: software/external reset requester
obligations.operation: trigger peripheral reset or observe state after reset
obligations.object: peripheral reset controls and residual crypto/fuse/RNG/RSA state
obligations.expected_guard: software-visible or external reset triggers for security-sensitive peripherals must be restricted by privilege/access policy and should fully clear protected state; reset controls should not be usable to bypass locks or leave stale outputs exposed
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals reset-control registers/we_flag_* -> rst_1..rst_13 -> wrappers (pkt rst_5, rng rst_10, rsa rst_13) and protected state clearing
state_conditions_to_check: reset default of protected registers and outputs
whether reset clears locks or policy registers
whether stale msg_out/seed/fuse data remains readable
whether reset can interrupt operation to bypass access policy
required_evidence_slots: subject: we_flag/control-register writer or reset NoC endpoint
operation: assert rst_* or reset peripheral
object: pkt/rng/rsa protected registers and outputs
expected guard: privilege/access control and complete clear semantics
observed behavior: reset generation and downstream reset use
obligations.uncertainty: Need line-level reset generation and wrapper sequential logic.
